key_evidence: HRW Apr 2 2026 ('Crimes Against Humanity by All Sides'): Burkina Faso military + allied militias killed 1,800+ civilians and forcibly displaced tens of thousands since 2023, including ethnic cleansing of Fulani civilians — amounting to crimes against humanity
HRW: drone strikes on crowded civilian markets (Bayraktar TB2 laser-guided munitions) ruled apparent war crimes; military acted 'with little or no concern for civilian harm'
HRW: junta curtails reporting to obscure civilian suffering (anti-transparency)
OCHA Apr 29 2026: 2,640 conflict deaths Jan-Mar 2026; 1,179 security incidents; 3M+ displaced; 7.5M children need aid
Multi-sided perpetration (JNIM also committing atrocities) + residual UN $1.16B Food Systems partnership distinguish from the 0.0 absolute floor
methodology_note: Active-state-perpetration reweight. The published uniform 1.5 (12.5) baseline predates HRW's Apr 2 2026 corroborated (Tier-5) determination of government crimes against humanity + ethnic cleansing of Fulani — the same multi-category active-perpetration conduct that anchors the 0.0-floor cases (Myanmar, Sudan). Downgrade concentrated in EQU/ACC/INT/EMP (deliberate ethnic targeting, atrocity denial, rhetoric-conduct contradiction); AWR/ACT/BND held marginally above floor (residual UN-partnered humanitarian capacity). Scored to 6.3, NOT 0.0, because the conflict is multi-sided (state not sole perpetrator) and limited state-UN humanitarian cooperation persists. No band crossing (Critical -> Critical). The Apr 2 HRW report was not reflected in the published baseline and was not scored in the 2026-05-21 first-baseline proposal (which focused on the junta-with-climate-leadership paradox and used a non-canonical 0-100 scale at composite 25.0).
